volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Captives (Section 2.2.260 - Section 2.2.354c)

sections.label: Section 2.2.260
sections.original: neque te nobis , sí abeamus hinc , si fuat occasio .
sections.translation: nor yet for you us , if we go away hence , should there be an opportunity .

sections.label: Section 2.2.261
sections.original: Vt vos hic , itidem illic apud vos meus servatur filius .
sections.translation: As you are here , so is my son a captive there among your people .

sections.label: Section 2.2.262
sections.original: Captus est ?
sections.translation: He , a captive ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.262b
sections.original: Ita .
sections.translation: Even so .

sections.label: Section 2.2.262c
sections.original: Non igitur nos soli ignavi fuimus .
sections.translation: We , then , have not proved the only cowards [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.263
sections.original: Secede huc . nam sunt quae éx te solo scitari volo .
sections.translation: Step you aside this way , for there are some things that I wish to enquire of you in private , on which subjects I would have you not to be untruthful to me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.264b
sections.original: Non ero
sections.translation: I will not be ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.265
sections.original: quod sciam . si quid nescibo , id nescium tradam tibi .
sections.translation: as to that which I shall know ; if I shall not know anything , that which I don ’ t know I ’ ll tell you of .

sections.label: Section 2.2.266
sections.original: Nunc senex est in tostrina , nunc iam cultros attinet .
sections.translation: Now is the old fellow in the barber ’ s shop ; now , at this very instant , is Philocrates wielding the razor [*] . He hasn ’ t cared , indeed , to put on the barber ’ s cloth [*] , so as not to soil his dress . But whether to say that he ’ s going to shave him close , or trim him [*] through the comb [*] , I don ’ t know ; but if he ’ s wise , he ’ ll scrape him right well to the very quick .

sections.label: Section 2.2.270
sections.original: Quid tu ? servosne esse an liber mavelis , memora mihi .
sections.translation: Which would you ? Would you prefer to be a slave , or a free man ?— Tell me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.271
sections.original: Proxumum quod sit bono quodque a malo longissume ,
sections.translation: That which is the nearest to good , and the furthest off from evil , do I prefer ; although my servitude hasn ’ t proved very grievous to me , nor has it been otherwise to me than if I had been a son in the family .

sections.label: Section 2.2.274
sections.original: Eugepae , Thalem talento non emam Milesium ,
sections.translation: Capital ! I wouldn ’ t purchase , at a talent ’ s price even , Thales the Milesian [*] ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.275
sections.original: nam ad sapientiam huius ⋯ nimius nugator fuit .
sections.translation: for compared with this man ’ s wisdom , he was a very twaddler . How cleverly has he suited his language to the slave ’ s condition .

sections.label: Section 2.2.277
sections.original: Quo de genere natust illic Philocrates ?
sections.translation: Of what family is this Philocrates born ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.277b
sections.original: Polyplusio :
sections.translation: The Polyplusian [*] ; which one family is flourishing there , and held in highest esteem .

sections.label: Section 2.2.279
sections.original: Quid ipsus hic ? quo honore est illic ?
sections.translation: What is he himself ? In what esteem is he held there ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.279b
sections.original: Summo , atque ab summis viris .
sections.translation: In the highest , and that by the very highest men .

sections.label: Section 2.2.280
sections.original: Tum igitur ei cum in Aleis tanta gratia est , ut praedicas ,
sections.translation: Since , then , he is held in such great respect among the Eleans , as you tell of , what substance has he ?— Of large amount ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.281b
sections.original: Vnde excoquat sebum senex .
sections.translation: Enough for him , even , when an old man , to be melting out the tallow [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.282
sections.original: Quid pater , vivitne ?
sections.translation: What is his father ? Is he living ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.282b
sections.original: Vivom , cum inde abimus , liquimus ;
sections.translation: When we departed thence , we left him alive ; whether he ’ s living now or not , Orcus , forsooth , must know that .

sections.label: Section 2.2.284
sections.original: Salva res est , philosophatur quoque iam , non mendax modo est .
sections.translation: The matter ’ s all right ; he ’ s not only lying , but he ’ s even philosophizing now .

sections.label: Section 2.2.285
sections.original: Quid erat ei nomen ?
sections.translation: What ’ s his name ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.285b
sections.original: Thensaurochrysonicochrysides .
sections.translation: Thesaurochrysonicocrœsides [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.286
sections.original: Videlicet propter divitias inditum id nomen quasi est .
sections.translation: That name has been given , I suppose , by reason of his wealth , as it were .

sections.label: Section 2.2.287
sections.original: Immo edepol propter avaritiam ipsius atque audaciam .
sections.translation: Troth , not so , but rather by reason of his avarice and grasping disposition ; for , indeed , he was Theodoromedes originally by name .

sections.label: Section 2.2.289
sections.original: Quid tu ais ? tenaxne pater est eius ?
sections.translation: How say you ? Is his father covetous ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.289b
sections.original: Immo edepol pertinax ;
sections.translation: Aye , by my faith , he is covetous .

sections.label: Section 2.2.290
sections.original: quin etiam ut magis nóscas : Genio suo ubi quando sacruficat ,
sections.translation: Why , that you may even understand it the better ,— when he ’ s sacrificing at any time to his own Genius [*] , the vessels that are needed for the sacrifice he uses of Samian ware , lest the Genius himself should steal them ; from this , consider how much he would trust other people .

sections.label: Section 2.2.293
sections.original: Sequere hac me igitur . eadem ego ex hoc quae volo exquaesivero .
sections.translation: Do you then follow me this way . ( Aside .) The things that I desire to know , I ’ ll enquire of him . ( Addressing TYNDARUS .) Philocrates , this person has done as it becomes an honest man to do .

sections.label: Section 2.2.295
sections.original: nám ego ex hoc quo genere gnatus sis scio , hic fassust mihi ;
sections.translation: For from him I ’ ve learnt of what family you are sprung ; he has confessed it to me . If you are willing to own these same things ( which , however , understand that I already know from him ), you will be doing it for your own advantage .

sections.label: Section 2.2.297b
sections.original: Fecit officium hic suom ,
sections.translation: He did his duty when he confessed the truth to you , although , Hegio , I wished carefully to conceal both my rank and my wealth ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.300
sections.original: Hegio ; nunc quando patriam et libertatem perdidi ,
sections.translation: now , inasmuch as I ’ ve lost my country and my liberty , I don ’ t think it right for him to be dreading me rather than you . The might of warfare has made my fortunes on a level with himself . I remember the time when he didn ’ t dare to do it in word ; now , in deed , he is at liberty to offend me . But don ’ t you see ? Human fortune moulds and fashions just as she wills .

sections.label: Section 2.2.305
sections.original: me , qui liber fueram , servom fecit , e summo infimum ;
sections.translation: Myself , who was a free man she has made a slave , from the very highest the very lowest . I , who was accustomed to command , now obey the mandates of another . And indeed , if I meet with a master just such as I proved the ruler in my own household , I shall not fear that he will rule me harshly or severely . With this , Hegio , I wished you to be acquainted , unless perchance you your self wish it not .

sections.label: Section 2.2.310
sections.original: Loquere audacter .
sections.translation: Speak boldly out .

sections.label: Section 2.2.310b
sections.original: Tam ego fui ante liber quam gnatus tuos ,
sections.translation: As free a man was I till lately as your son . As much did a hostile hand deprive me of my liberty as him of his . As much is he a slave among my people , as I am now a slave here with yourself . There is undoubtedly a God , who both hears and sees the things which we do . Just as you shall treat me here , in the same degree will he have a care for him .

sections.label: Section 2.2.315
sections.original: bene merenti bene profuerit , male merenti par erit .
sections.translation: To the well-deserving will he show favour , to the ill-deserving will he give a like return . As much as you lament your son , so much does my father lament me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.317
sections.original: Memini ego istuc . sed faterin eadem quae hic fassust mihi ?
sections.translation: That I am aware of . But do you admit the same that lie has disclosed to me ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.318
sections.original: Ego patri meo esse fateor summas divitias domi
sections.translation: I confess that my father has very great wealth at home , and that I am born of a very noble family ; but I entreat you , Hegio ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.320
sections.original: ne tuom animum avariorem faxint divitiae meae :
sections.translation: let not my riches make your mind too prone to avarice , lest it should seem to my father , although I am his only son , more suitable that I should be a slave in your house , bountifully supplied at your expense and with your clothing , rather than be living the life of a beggar where ‘ twould be far from honorable .

sections.label: Section 2.2.324
sections.original: Ego virtute deum et maiorum nostrum dives sum satis .
sections.translation: By the favour of the Gods and of my forefathers , I am rich enough .

sections.label: Section 2.2.325
sections.original: non ego ómnino lucrum omne esse utile homini existimo :
sections.translation: I don ’ t quite believe that every kind of gain is serviceable to mankind . I know that gain has already made many a man famous ; and yet there are occasions when it is undoubtedly better to incur loss than to make gain . Gold I detest : many a one has it persuaded to many an evil course . Now give your attention to this , that you may know as well what my wishes are .

sections.label: Section 2.2.330
sections.original: filius meus illic apud vos servit captus Alide :
sections.translation: My son , taken prisoner . is in servitude at Elis there among your people ; if you restore him to me , don ’ t you give me a single coin besides ; both you and him , your servant , I ’ ll send back from here ; on no other terms can you depart hence .

sections.label: Section 2.2.333
sections.original: Optumum atque aequissumum oras optumusque hominum es homo .
sections.translation: You ask what ’ s very right and very just , and you are the very kindest person of all mankind . But whether is he in servitude to a private person or to the public [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.335
sections.original: Privatam medici Menarchi .
sections.translation: In private servitude to Menarchus , a physician .

sections.label: Section 2.2.335b
sections.original: Pol is quidem huius est cliens .
sections.translation: By my faith , that person ’ s surely his father ’ s dependant . Why really , that ’ s down as pat for you , as the shower is when it rains .

sections.label: Section 2.2.337
sections.original: Fac is homo ut redimatur .
sections.translation: Do you then cause this person , my son , to be redeemed .

sections.label: Section 2.2.337b
sections.original: Faciam . sed te id oro , Hegio —
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so : but this I beg of you , Hegio —

sections.label: Section 2.2.338
sections.original: Quid vis , dum ab re ne quid ores , faciam .
sections.translation: Whatever you wish , so that you request nothing against my interest , I ’ ll do .

sections.label: Section 2.2.338b
sections.original: Ausculta , tum scies .
sections.translation: Listen then , and you ’ ll know . I don ’ t ask for myself to be released , until he has returned .

sections.label: Section 2.2.340
sections.original: verum quaeso ut aestumatum hunc mihi des , quem mittam ad patrem ,
sections.translation: But I beg of you to give me him ( pointing to PHILOCRATES ) with a price set [*] upon him , that I may send him to my father , that this person , your son , may be redeemed there .

sections.label: Section 2.2.341b
sections.original: Immo alium potius misero
sections.translation: Why no ; I ’ d rather send another person hence , when there shall be a truce , to confer with your father there , and to carry your injunctions which you shall entrust him with , just as you wish .

sections.label: Section 2.2.344
sections.original: At nihil est ignotum ad illum mittere : operam luseris .
sections.translation: But it ’ s of no use to send to him one that he doesn ’ t know ; you ’ d be losing your labour .

sections.label: Section 2.2.345
sections.original: hunc mitte , hic transactum reddet omne , si illuc venerit .
sections.translation: Send this person ; he ’ ll have it all completed , if he gets there . And you cannot send any person to him more faithful , nor one in whom he places more confidence , nor who is more a servant after his own mind ; nor , in fact , one to whom he would more readily entrust your son . Have no fears ; at my own peril I ’ ll make proof of his fidelity ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.350
sections.original: fretus ingenio eius , quod me ésse scit erga se benevolum .
sections.translation: relying upon his disposition ; because he is sensible that I ’ m kindly disposed towards him .

sections.label: Section 2.2.351
sections.original: Mittam equidem istunc aestumatum tua fide , si vis .
sections.translation: Well then , I ’ ll send him with a price set upon him , on the surety of your promise , if you wish it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.351b
sections.original: Volo ;
sections.translation: I do wish it ; so soon as ever it can , I want this matter to be brought to completion .

sections.label: Section 2.2.353
sections.original: Num quae causa est quin , si ille huc non redeat , viginti minas
sections.translation: What reason is there , then , that if he doesn ’ t return , you should not pay me twenty minae for him ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.354b
sections.original: Optuma immo .
sections.translation: Yes — very good .

sections.label: Section 2.2.354c
sections.original: Solvite istum nunciam ,
sections.translation: Release him now forthwith ;
